title: Introduce GPU ephemeral backup tfmodule and tfstate anchoring helper
preview:
Background and motivation:
This PR introduces two small, composable terraform modules to reduce operational risk during Private installations that use GPU node pools and sharded tfstate layouts. We've seen recurring incidents where control-plane drift or partial apply failures during nodepool upgrades required manua...
text:
Background and motivation:
This PR introduces two small, composable terraform modules to reduce operational risk during Private installations that use GPU node pools and sharded tfstate layouts. We've seen recurring incidents where control-plane drift or partial apply failures during nodepool upgrades required manual state surgeries (ENG-4821). The goal is to provide a repeatable, automated safety net: ephemeral backups of nodepool metadata and a lightweight tfstate anchoring helper that produces a signed checkpoint file distributed to the ops bucket.

What this PR does (summary):
- Adds modules/gpu_ephemeral_backup: an opt-in module that creates a snapshot of the nodepool control metadata, saves kubelet/CRI config snippets, and records a lightweight manifest (node labels, taints, instance-ids) to the configured artifacts bucket. Designed for very short retention (24–72h) to minimize ...

title: Marketplace provisioning: IAM + webhook config for entitlement sync (least privilege)
preview: Context: Marketplace-driven customers need a reliable subscription → entitlement → org provisioning flow. The control-plane entitlement sync service now consumes entitlement signals from both AWS Marketplace (API polling + optional notifications) and GCP Marketplace (vendor webhook). This PR adds Terraform-managed I...
text: Context: Marketplace-driven customers need a reliable subscription → entitlement → org provisioning flow. The control-plane entitlement sync service now consumes entitlement signals from both AWS Marketplace (API polling + optional notifications) and GCP Marketplace (vendor webhook). This PR adds Terraform-managed IAM, network, and webhook plumbing needed by the entitlement sync services while keeping permissions least-privilege and auditable. Changes: (1) AWS IAM: new dedicated IAM role for entitlement-sync with scoped permissions to aws-marketplace entitlement + metering APIs; separate role for webhook verifier to read secrets and write limited logs/metrics; explicit deny for broad S3 and KMS actions; (2) Webhook ingress: add an internal ALB listener rule + path-based routing for /marketplace/gcp/webhook to the entitlement-sync service target group; add WAF rule set and request size...

title: perf-canary: wire service account, secret storage, and least-privilege IAM
preview: Summary: This PR adds the Terraform wiring needed to deploy perf-canary safely: (1) a dedicated service account with least-privilege IAM, (2) secret storage + access policies for canary auth, and (3) environment/region guardrails to prevent accidental fanout. This unblocks the perf-canary Helm chart rollout by provi...
text:
Summary: This PR adds the Terraform wiring needed to deploy perf-canary safely: (1) a dedicated service account with least-privilege IAM, (2) secret storage + access policies for canary auth, and (3) environment/region guardrails to prevent accidental fanout. This unblocks the perf-canary Helm chart rollout by providing a constrained identity that can call internal inference endpoints and emit metrics without broad access.

Context: perf-canary runs synthetic workloads against internal inference endpoints and publishes performance metrics/alerts. We need a dedicated identity for the canary deployment (no shared runtime/telemetry SA), scoped to: read-only inference invoke, write-only metrics, and read-only baseline artifact access. We also need to store the canary auth token / client secret in managed secret storage and allow only the canary workload identity to decrypt/access it.

Wha...

title: DNS TTL tuning + routing primitives for faster regional failover
preview: Summary: This PR updates Terraform for Redwood’s cross-region traffic steering so regional failover can converge faster and more predictably during impairment. The main change is lowering DNS TTLs for the API entrypoints used by Hosted API and Dedicated (where applicable), while adding guardrails to avoid unintended...
text:
Summary: This PR updates Terraform for Redwood’s cross-region traffic steering so regional failover can converge faster and more predictably during impairment. The main change is lowering DNS TTLs for the API entrypoints used by Hosted API and Dedicated (where applicable), while adding guardrails to avoid unintended traffic shifts (provider caching variance, resolver behavior, and over-eager health checks). It also adds explicit routing primitives (health checks + failover policy wiring + outputs for the failover controller) so the automation controller can rely on consistent infra definitions across environments.

Motivation / context: Regional failover automation upgrades (Reliability Program) require that DNS-based steering not be the long pole. Today, several records use TTLs that are appropriate for steady-state but extend failover time, and some resources are defined inconsisten...

title: Refresh Terraform modules CI/CD pipeline and integration tests
preview: Context: Our Confluence CI/CD page for redwood-terraform describes the original alpha pipeline. Since then we’ve added more modules/examples and the integration suite has been flakier and harder to reason about (provider init duplication, inconsistent path filtering, unclear when to run AWS ephemeral tests). This PR...
text: Context: Our Confluence CI/CD page for redwood-terraform describes the original alpha pipeline. Since then we’ve added more modules/examples and the integration suite has been flakier and harder to reason about (provider init duplication, inconsistent path filtering, unclear when to run AWS ephemeral tests). This PR refreshes the GitHub Actions workflows and updates the repo’s CI guidance to reflect the current reality. Summary of changes: (1) PR validation now uses a single module/example discovery step that drives a matrix for fmt/validate/tflint and avoids re-running init unnecessarily. (2) Security scanning: keep Checkov as the baseline gate but also upload Trivy IaC (config) results to SARIF for additional signal; Checkov remains the merge-blocking policy for now. (3) Integration tests: previously nightly + manual; now we also auto-run integration tests on PRs when risk classifie...

title: Expose Dedicated scaling policy (min/max bounds + predictive mode) in modules and examples
preview: This PR updates the redwood-terraform modules and example configs to support the new Dedicated autoscaling policy fields (customer-configurable min/max bounds + predictive mode toggle). The provider/resource schema changes landed in the redwood monorepo; this PR focuses on surfacing the knobs in our Terraform module...
text: This PR updates the redwood-terraform modules and example configs to support the new Dedicated autoscaling policy fields (customer-configurable min/max bounds + predictive mode toggle). The provider/resource schema changes landed in the redwood monorepo; this PR focuses on surfacing the knobs in our Terraform modules, keeping backward compatibility (no behavior change unless fields are set), and documenting expected semantics + feature-flag requirements for predictive mode. Key updates: (1) Dedicated module: add optional inputs for scaling policy (min_replicas, max_replicas, scale_up_cooldown_seconds, scale_down_cooldown_seconds, predictive_mode, predictive_lead_time_seconds). (2) Examples: add a dedicated_deployment example showing cost ceiling + baseline capacity, and an example enabling predictive mode for known daily ramps. (3) Docs: README updates describing precedence (bounds cl...

title: Add release tagging and changelog generation
preview: Summary: This PR introduces an opinionated release workflow for redwood-terraform so we can cut alpha module versions consistently (tags + changelog + lightweight automation) without relying on ad-hoc manual steps. This is intended to support the VPC Terraform modules (alpha) program and aligns with the module versi...
text: Summary: This PR introduces an opinionated release workflow for redwood-terraform so we can cut alpha module versions consistently (tags + changelog + lightweight automation) without relying on ad-hoc manual steps. This is intended to support the VPC Terraform modules (alpha) program and aligns with the module versioning/release process doc. Motivation: We’ve started landing a steady stream of alpha changes (VPC, endpoints, flow logs, IAM scaffold, CI). Field teams and design partners need pinned versions and readable deltas. Today, we’re manually tagging and writing release notes, and the process is already diverging between maintainers. Goals: (1) One button / one command path to cut a release; (2) deterministic tags (vX.Y.Z); (3) generated CHANGELOG entries based on merged PR metadata; (4) minimal GitHub token scope; (5) guardrails to avoid releasing from dirty or non-main states. ...

title: Add optional VPN/DX placeholders and routing hooks (no provider assumptions)
preview:
Title: Add optional VPN/DX placeholders and routing hooks (no provider assumptions)

Context
Design partners keep asking, "Where do we plug in our VPN / Direct Connect / Transit Gateway routes?" Our alpha modules intentionally avoid building VPN/DX/TGW resources (provider + enterprise topology specific), but we also...
text:
Title: Add optional VPN/DX placeholders and routing hooks (no provider assumptions)

Context
Design partners keep asking, "Where do we plug in our VPN / Direct Connect / Transit Gateway routes?" Our alpha modules intentionally avoid building VPN/DX/TGW resources (provider + enterprise topology specific), but we also need to stop forcing customers to fork the VPC module just to add a couple of routes.

This PR adds:
- Stable outputs for route table IDs and subnet groupings so customers can attach routes externally (VPN/TGW/DX) without modifying our modules.
- Optional, generic "routing hooks" inputs for adding additional routes to the private/public route tables when the target is an ID we already know (e.g., a pre-existing TGW attachment, VGW, or NAT instance) while keeping the module free of assumptions about what creates that target.
- Docs + example showing recommended patterns (cu...

title: CI: add tflint + checkov gates; pin Terraform/AWS provider versions for reproducible builds
preview:
Summary
This PR hardens the `redwood-terraform` alpha module repo by adding Terraform linting + security scanning in CI and by pinning Terraform/AWS provider versions to improve reproducibility across dev machines and CI runners.

Motivation
We’ve had a few sources of drift already during dogfood: different AWS prov...
text:
Summary
This PR hardens the `redwood-terraform` alpha module repo by adding Terraform linting + security scanning in CI and by pinning Terraform/AWS provider versions to improve reproducibility across dev machines and CI runners.

Motivation
We’ve had a few sources of drift already during dogfood: different AWS provider patch versions producing slightly different plans, and “looks fine locally” modules that fail basic best-practice checks once they hit customer environments. Since these modules are explicitly meant to encode Redwood-validated defaults, we should catch issues early and keep outputs stable.

What’s included
- Provider + Terraform version pinning:
 - Add explicit `required_version` and `required_providers` constraints across modules/examples.
 - Standardize on AWS provider `~> 5.0` with a minimum patch level validated in sandbox.
 - Add/refresh `.terraform.lock.hcl` for ...

title: Add integration test harness for ephemeral VPC (create/validate/destroy)
preview: Summary: This PR introduces an integration test harness that provisions an ephemeral AWS VPC using our alpha modules, validates a small set of expected invariants/outputs, and then tears everything down. The goal is to catch provider/module drift and unsafe defaults earlier than design partner pilots (e.g., subnet m...
text: Summary: This PR introduces an integration test harness that provisions an ephemeral AWS VPC using our alpha modules, validates a small set of expected invariants/outputs, and then tears everything down. The goal is to catch provider/module drift and unsafe defaults earlier than design partner pilots (e.g., subnet mapping, NAT assumptions, endpoint/DNS regressions). Motivation/Context: Unit-level terraform validate/tflint/checkov are necessary but not sufficient; we’ve already had issues that only show up once AWS resources exist (e.g., AZ assumptions, route association wiring, endpoint private DNS behavior). This harness is a minimal “smoke” environment that deploys modules the way customers will via examples, with assertions for the outputs the installer expects downstream. What’s included: (1) A Go-based Terratest suite under test/integration/ that runs terraform init/apply against...

title: Add VPC Flow Logs defaults + configurable log destinations (CloudWatch/S3)
preview:
Title: Add VPC Flow Logs defaults + configurable log destinations (CloudWatch/S3)

Context
We’ve had repeated pilot friction around “are we capturing network telemetry by default, and where does it go?” For Redwood Private VPC deployments we want sane, audit-friendly defaults while still letting customers route logs...
text:
Title: Add VPC Flow Logs defaults + configurable log destinations (CloudWatch/S3)

Context
We’ve had repeated pilot friction around “are we capturing network telemetry by default, and where does it go?” For Redwood Private VPC deployments we want sane, audit-friendly defaults while still letting customers route logs to their own sinks (CloudWatch or S3 today; SIEM forwarding remains out-of-scope for these modules).

This PR introduces a new flow logs submodule and wires it into the example envs:
- Default-on VPC Flow Logs for the created VPC
- Destination options: CloudWatch Logs or S3 (customer-owned bucket supported)
- Explicit retention/lifecycle inputs with conservative defaults and clear cost/audit trade-offs

What changed
1) New module: modules/flow-logs
- Creates aws_flow_log for the VPC
- Supports traffic_type = ALL by default (input override allowed)
- Destination selection:
...

title: Add IAM scaffold for Redwood Private installer execution role (with permission boundaries)
preview:
## Summary
This PR introduces an alpha IAM scaffolding module to support running `redwood-private-installer` in customer AWS accounts using a dedicated execution role. The module creates an IAM role + inline policy (or wires to an existing role), and is designed to be compatible with customer permission boundary req...
text:
## Summary
This PR introduces an alpha IAM scaffolding module to support running `redwood-private-installer` in customer AWS accounts using a dedicated execution role. The module creates an IAM role + inline policy (or wires to an existing role), and is designed to be compatible with customer permission boundary requirements from day one.

The goal is not to encode every enterprise IAM topology, but to provide a Redwood-validated default that works for most pilots and gives security teams a clear review surface.

## Why
During discovery/pilots we repeatedly hit delays around: (1) "what IAM does the installer need?" and (2) "how do we enforce least privilege and boundaries?". Providing a standardized scaffold reduces iteration time and makes the rest of the VPC module suite easier to adopt.

## What changed
- New module: `modules/iam_installer_execution_role`
 - Creates `aws_iam_role.i...

title: Add VPC module with subnet + routing defaults
preview: Summary: This PR implements the core `modules/vpc` module for the Private VPC Terraform modules (alpha). The goal is to provide a Redwood-validated default VPC layout suitable for Redwood Private deployments that run on EKS (assumed) with private worker nodes and conservative routing defaults. This module focuses on...
text: Summary: This PR implements the core `modules/vpc` module for the Private VPC Terraform modules (alpha). The goal is to provide a Redwood-validated default VPC layout suitable for Redwood Private deployments that run on EKS (assumed) with private worker nodes and conservative routing defaults. This module focuses on: VPC creation, public/private subnet layout across AZs, route tables, and a NAT strategy with explicit trade-offs documented. Motivation: Technical discovery/pilots keep getting blocked on basic networking variability (subnet layouts, route tables, NAT/egress assumptions). We want a default that works for most enterprise VPC baselines while remaining composable with future modules (endpoints/logging/iam) and customer-specific connectivity (VPN/DX) without forcing premature complexity. What’s included: (1) VPC with DNS enabled by default: `enable_dns_support=true` and `enab...

title: Add VPC endpoints module + private DNS options for Redwood Private (alpha)
preview:
## Summary
This adds an opinionated `vpc-endpoints` Terraform module for the Redwood Private VPC footprint (alpha). The module creates a baseline set of Gateway + Interface endpoints commonly required in fully-private subnets (no public egress) and exposes explicit inputs for private DNS behavior.

Primary motivatio...
text:
## Summary
This adds an opinionated `vpc-endpoints` Terraform module for the Redwood Private VPC footprint (alpha). The module creates a baseline set of Gateway + Interface endpoints commonly required in fully-private subnets (no public egress) and exposes explicit inputs for private DNS behavior.

Primary motivation: reduce pilot failures related to image pulls / registry access / AWS API access in private subnets by making endpoints part of our reference modules, while staying compatible with customer DNS strategies (Route 53 Resolver, custom forwarders, split-horizon, etc.).

## What’s included
### New module: `modules/vpc-endpoints`
Creates endpoints in the provided VPC + subnets:
- **Gateway endpoint**: S3 (`com.amazonaws.<region>.s3`) with route table association(s)
- **Interface endpoints** (baseline):
 - ECR API (`com.amazonaws.<region>.ecr.api`)
 - ECR DKR (`com.amazonaws.<re...

title: Add security group patterns for private ingress/egress (EKS + installer-friendly outputs)
preview: This PR introduces an opinionated set of Security Group (SG) modules/patterns for Redwood Private AWS deployments (alpha). The intent is to reduce pilot variability and common discovery churn by shipping Redwood-validated defaults for: (1) EKS worker/node SG behavior (private nodes), (2) load balancer SGs for intern...
text: This PR introduces an opinionated set of Security Group (SG) modules/patterns for Redwood Private AWS deployments (alpha). The intent is to reduce pilot variability and common discovery churn by shipping Redwood-validated defaults for: (1) EKS worker/node SG behavior (private nodes), (2) load balancer SGs for internal ingress (ALB/NLB) and controlled cross-subnet traffic, and (3) egress policy that works in a mostly-private VPC with VPC endpoints (ECR/S3/STS/CloudWatch Logs, etc.). Key goals: keep the default posture secure (no accidental broad public egress), but avoid breaking real-world installs where customers still need proxy/NAT-based egress or have non-standard endpoint/DNS setups. The modules are designed to be composable and to expose controlled extension points (additional egress CIDRs, additional destination SGs, and an explicit opt-in for “wide egress”). Summary of changes...

title: Private: add version pinning, upgrade window controls, and artifact source configuration
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds explicit Terraform inputs for Redwood Private lifecycle management: (1) version pinning (N-1 → N upgrade default), (2) upgrade window configuration to encode operator intent and reduce accidental upgrades outside of maintenance windows, and (3) artifact source configuration to make artifact p...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds explicit Terraform inputs for Redwood Private lifecycle management: (1) version pinning (N-1 → N upgrade default), (2) upgrade window configuration to encode operator intent and reduce accidental upgrades outside of maintenance windows, and (3) artifact source configuration to make artifact provenance explicit (registry/chart repo URLs) and unblock future offline mirror work.

These changes are intentionally additive and default to today’s behavior when unset.

## Motivation
We’re rolling out a safer, more deterministic Private upgrade workflow (plan + prechecks + backup/rollback). Terraform is a primary entry point for VPC Private installs, and we need a consistent way to:
- Pin the target Redwood Private platform version across environments
- Avoid accidental upgrades by making a maintenance window an explicit input (and validated when provided)
- Standardize...

title: Add Terraform modules for Dedicated burst pools (per region + hardware class)
preview: Context: Dedicated Burst Capacity needs region/hardware-scoped “burst pools” that can be independently sized and rolled out (e.g., us-east/H100, eu-west/L40S), with clear safety controls and consistent tagging/monitoring. This PR adds reusable Terraform modules + environment wiring to provision burst pools per regio...
text:
Context: Dedicated Burst Capacity needs region/hardware-scoped “burst pools” that can be independently sized and rolled out (e.g., us-east/H100, eu-west/L40S), with clear safety controls and consistent tagging/monitoring. This PR adds reusable Terraform modules + environment wiring to provision burst pools per region/hardware class behind allow-lists, and to standardize alerts/labels used by the admission controller + SRE dashboards.

What’s in this PR: (1) New module: modules/burst_pool (GPU pool + autoscaler + quotas/labels). The module provisions a dedicated node pool / capacity group for “managed burst” with explicit isolation labels/taints so only burst workloads land there. Supports both “shared burst pool” (multi-tenant) and “segmented burst pool” (by tier) via a pool_id + tier label. (2) New module: modules/burst_pool_observability to attach baseline monitors and alert routes ...

title: Add retention policy config resources/modules for Private deployments
preview: Summary: Adds Terraform resources and a small helper module to configure retention controls for Redwood Private deployments where the control plane supports the retention policy service (policy schema v1.1). This is intended to reduce Security/GRC friction during enterprise onboarding by making retention configurati...
text: Summary: Adds Terraform resources and a small helper module to configure retention controls for Redwood Private deployments where the control plane supports the retention policy service (policy schema v1.1). This is intended to reduce Security/GRC friction during enterprise onboarding by making retention configuration auditable, repeatable, and exportable. Motivation: Several Private design partners requested “infrastructure as code” for retention defaults and per-tenant overrides to align with internal change control. This PR wires up policy CRUD via the retention policy API and provides safe-by-default validation consistent with the control plane schema (min/max TTLs per tier enforced server-side; Terraform additionally validates basic shape for faster feedback). Scope: - New resource: redwood_retention_policy (create/update/delete + read) - New resource: redwood_retention_policy_at...

title: Add NFS backup backend example + mount option guardrails
preview:
Summary: This PR adds a first-class Terraform example for configuring NFS-backed backups for Redwood Private, and introduces input validation guardrails to prevent insecure NFS mount options from being used in production-like environments.

Context / Why: We’re seeing an increasing number of on-prem / restricted cus...
text:
Summary: This PR adds a first-class Terraform example for configuring NFS-backed backups for Redwood Private, and introduces input validation guardrails to prevent insecure NFS mount options from being used in production-like environments.

Context / Why: We’re seeing an increasing number of on-prem / restricted customers (and a few VPC installs) asking for NFS as their backup artifact store (either because object storage is not available, or because backup traffic must remain fully local). In parallel, we’ve had a few staging misconfigs where the backup CronJob mounted an NFS share with overly-permissive mount options (e.g., allowing suid/dev/exec), which is a footgun for environments that run backup tooling with elevated privileges. This PR is intended to (a) provide a reference implementation customers can copy/paste and (b) enforce sane defaults / fail-fast validation.

What’s inc...

title: Add S3+KMS backup module for Redwood Private (bucket, key, least-privilege IAM) + examples
preview: Context: Private Day-2 backup/restore requires a supported, repeatable storage + encryption + IAM pattern for VPC deployments. This PR adds a Terraform module that provisions an S3 bucket configured for encrypted backup artifacts (SSE-KMS), a dedicated KMS key (optionally using an existing key), and a least-privileg...
text: Context: Private Day-2 backup/restore requires a supported, repeatable storage + encryption + IAM pattern for VPC deployments. This PR adds a Terraform module that provisions an S3 bucket configured for encrypted backup artifacts (SSE-KMS), a dedicated KMS key (optionally using an existing key), and a least-privilege IAM policy intended for the backup/restore job/installer identity. The module is designed to be "drop-in" for enterprise VPC environments with restricted IAM, and to align with the backup artifact layout produced by `redwood-private-installer backup create` (manifest + encrypted blob). What’s included: (1) module `modules/private_backup_s3_kms/` with S3 bucket, bucket policy options, versioning/lifecycle hooks, and KMS key + alias; (2) IAM policy document for backup writers/readers scoped to the bucket and a configurable prefix (default `redwood-private/backups/`), includ...
